description_long: This object is a solar energy infrastructure installation consisting of a rectangular array of solar panels mounted on a metallic frame above a ribbed, flat surface that resembles a rooftop or platform. The solar panels are dark, likely monocrystalline or polycrystalline silicon, and are supported by a sturdy, angled metal structure that elevates them above the base. The frame appears to be made of aluminum or galvanized steel, providing both support and resistance to weather. The base platform is patterned with alternating light and dark stripes, suggesting a corrugated metal or composite material, typical of urban or industrial rooftops. The installation is designed for optimal sunlight exposure, with the panels tilted at an angle. No visible electrical inverters or cabling are present in the images, and the structure is fixed in place, intended for long-term use.
description: A rooftop solar panel array mounted on a striped, metallic platform with a tilted metal support frame. The installation is robust and designed for fixed, urban placement.
description_view_0: Front view showing the solar panel array elevated above the striped platform, with the supporting frame visible beneath the panels.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the tilt of the solar panels and the supporting frame's angled legs, as well as the shadow cast on the platform.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the solar panels and the full width of the striped platform base.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the tilt angle of the panels and the supporting structure, with the platform extending to the right.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 25 degrees, azimuth direction not clearly specified, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: roof-mounted on a ribbed platform with an aluminum or steel angled frame
attribute_array_configuration: single row of 2 large rectangular panels mounted side by side
